programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate a hotel frequented by foreign diplomats after a woman's body is found wrapped in bedsheets. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The first instalment of a two-part crossover with NCIS: New Orleans sees the Washington, DC team co-operating with their colleagues from the Big Easy to investigate a spate of lethal poisonings on board a New Orleans-to-DC flight. All the evidence seems to point to missing chef Luca, who happens to be Abby's brother, so the two investigative services join forces to locate him, and find out why a private tech firm seems to have been targetted by the killer. Naval crime drama, guest starring Scott Bakula

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Kensi and the team hunt for an electromagnetic pulse device that was stolen from a college research facility, and could destroy Los Angeles if activated, killing millions of people. Elsewhere, Sam is on the lookout for a Christmas present for his daughter. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell, LL Cool J and Daniela Ruah

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Marine is seemingly killed in a mortar attack on America's heavily fortified administrative HQ in Baghdad, but a subsequent autopsy reveals he was murdered. Meanwhile, newcomer Beth Taylor gives Eric and Tinker a helping hand. Ian McShane stars

programme.desc: A vicar suffers a nervous breakdown at a resident's funeral and Tom's son has trouble with his nymphomaniac wife. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard concocts a plan to allay Pearl's well-founded suspicions - he just needs to find someone as small as himself. Comedy, starring Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Brian Wilde

Amanda Redman stars

programme.desc: A man researching a guidebook on cross-country walks foolishly accepts Foggy's offer of help, hoping the self-proclaimed survival expert's military training ensures his expedition runs like clockwork. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad is ordered by its new boss to look into the unsolved murder of a barrister found bound and gagged in his car 25 years ago. The case was a rare failure for a former chief inspector, although his notes point to a very clear suspect. As they uncover the late advocate's unusual hobbies, they gradually learn exactly what happened. Anita Dobson, Jenny Agutter and Keith Barron guest star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki and Jack attend the scene of a fatal car crash in which a driver lost control of her vehicle and the forensic evidence suggests it may not have been an accident and that the victim's son Kevin McDowd - a resident of Kingscote Bows care home - could be involved in the death. The teams begin to notice a pattern of foul play and a connection between the care home and other suspicious deaths that had previously gone unnoticed, while Kevin absconds and takes his friend Serena with him

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: After a game of poker with friends, Jack is horrified to see a speeding motorist deliberately run over one of the party - his godson Kieron - and jumps to the conclusion he must have been the intended target of a hit-and-run attack. As his junior officers begin to pick over the relationships within the group, it emerges Jack does not know his friends as well as he thought. John Vine guest stars

programme.desc: Niagara Falls beckons for actor John Pierce Jones and his travelling companion Dilwyn Morgan on their road trip across America - a natural wonder both have wanted to visit since they were children. After experiencing the Falls up close, they swap the camper van for a canal boat and spend a few days traveling America's iconic Erie Canal. While on the waterways, John faces his fears by white water kayaking and even attempts docking the canal boat on his own which does not go as smoothly as Dilwyn would have liked

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd
programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Welsh Whisperer - Ni'n Teithio Nawr
programme.desc: Andrew Walton, aka the Welsh Whisperer, travels to Carmarthenshire's largest village - Pontyberem. Local bard Aneirin Karadog talks about village life and the true meaning behind the village's name

programme.title: Noson Lawen
programme.desc: Mari Grug presents an evening of entertainment with artists from Powys. With Rhys Gwynfor a'r band, Rhodri Prys Jones, Robert Lewis, Ieuan Jones, Lisa Angharad, Osian Llewelyn, Cas Jones and Cadi Glwys Davies

programme.title: Bregus
programme.desc: After confessing to her affair with Richard and leaving Mart and their family home, Ellie receives a message from an unknown number - it's a picture of her and Ems kissing. She knows that the game is far from over and if she's not going to lose everything she's going to have to fight back and fast - but what more could Richard possibly want from her?

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of properties in Carlisle in Cumbria, London's Forest Gate and Walsall in the West Midlands

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins the East Yorkshire bus company in Hull, getting behind the wheel of a double decker, making sure the vehicles go out on time and helping to keep them in perfect working order

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Paisley, paramedics Gary and Stephen help a patient with a rare autoimmune disease who has lost mobility. In Edinburgh, Moira and Blair treat a man with severe abdominal pain

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items at Newark Antiques and Collectors Fair in Nottinghamshire, and presenter Charlie Ross learns about the Royal Observer Corps

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie kickstarts her investigation to track down the baby from the photo, while Rob is not too happy about the new sergeant at the police station. Jimmi tries to help a young lad with a stammer who has vital information for the police but is struggling to share it

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple of keen horticulturists who have £450,000 to find a home in the Yorkshire Dales, and also visits RHS Garden Harlow Carr to meet Alan Titchmarsh

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore explores Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, discovering a set of dining chairs with a matching table, a set of vintage lawn games, and some old aluminium lamp-shades

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea takes on the restoration of a once brightly coloured wooden carousel horse, while ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay is charged with the task of repairing a little cat teapot with an extraordinary wartime story behind it. Plus, Will Kirk takes on the repair of a 3D wooden boat puzzle

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By UKIP

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Kathy from Leicester presents the dealers with a catalogue for an auction to help people who lost loved ones in the Titanic disaster. Alice from Wales presents then with an unusual moulded men's underwear advert from the early 20th century - although the dealers are not so sure when they realise a bit is missing - and James from Stoke-on-Trent hopes to sell a pair of Victorian horse hair singers. Presented by Nigel Havers, with expert Simon Bower

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the experts get to work on more heirlooms to bring them, and the memories they hold, back to life. Dominic Chinea faces the heavy-duty task of repairing a seized-up retro roundabout that reminds its owner Stella of her three daughters' childhoods. Wood expert Will Kirk is amazed when a First World War dominoes set, thought to have been played in the Christmas truce of 1914, arrives in the barn. Suzie Fletcher uses her leather skills on a 19th-century women's side saddle bought in Texas, and Julie and Amanda need the help of mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher to fix a broken teddy bear

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's gentlemen's classics week, and the sewers' skills are tested to the limit as they tackle headwear - a first for the show - in the shape of a Peaky Blinders-style baker boy cap, before being given 90 minutes to smarten up and transform second-hand men's jackets and blazers into a garment for a woman. In the made-to-measure challenge, they must create a casual utility jacket, with a relaxed fit and at least three pockets. Joe Lycett follows their progress

programme.title: Is Uni Racist?
programme.desc: Britain's universities are some of the most prestigious in the world, but are they safe places for students of colour? Linda Adey investigates the experiences of black and ethnic minority students and examines what happens when victims of racist abuse at university want to complain

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ronan Keating, as they assist a father and son from Crawley in picking out the best singers - and get a few dance lessons in return

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Wolverhampton to see a three-bed, end-of-terrace house with scope to extend. He's also in Willenhall in the West Midlands to visit a bungalow which might offer more potential as a new building plot. In Norfolk, Tommy Walsh visits a three-bed house that he's a big fan of

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood takes a walk on the wild side, working as a zookeeper at the largest zoo in the UK. As he mucks in, he discovers that the zoo's conservation efforts are helping make sure that many of these endangered species survive in the wild for generations to come

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations paramedic Brian is called to help his colleagues at a bus crash in Glasgow that has left passengers injured. Near Peterhead, an elderly patient who has difficulty moving, needs to be transferred to hospital, so ambulance crew members Ricky and Luan use their skills to help him into the vehicle

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts as experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items. The presenter also receives a visit by the chairman of the Torquay Pottery Collectors Society, who has a rather special pasty to show him

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: When a proud mother faces her own mortality, she opens up a can of worms that threatens to destroy her relationship with her daughter. Valerie is delighted when Ricky Delaine propositions her, while Rob and Harriet start to bond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Eighteen contestants remain in the running to win £10,000, including Praveen, Chris, Melanie and Gareth - aware that a wrong answer at any stage will see them knocked out

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley showcases an arresting range of houses to a couple who have a substantial budget of £700,000 and are hoping to relocate their family to the Norfolk countryside. For over half a century, Norfolk has been home to Lotus cars, so while in the county Ginny finds out more about the company heritage before catching a glimpse of the future when their latest hypercar is unveiled

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore, upholsterer Simion Hawtin-Smith and print artist Zoe Murphy breathe new life into a retro swivel chair, a large oak dining table and a once-cherished sideboard

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Radio expert Mark Stuckey has a job on his hands, repairing a 1930s transistor radio brought into the Repair Shop by David Hill from Brecon. The radio was given to David's father by an army officer on the beaches at Dunkirk during the Second World War, and after being successfully shepherded through the bullets and the crashing waves to safety, it became the Hill family's pride and joy. However, the subsequent years have not been kind, with the vintage transistor battered and broken both inside and out. A battered leather gaming case and a grandfather clock with a poignant history are also given a new lease of life

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: An election broadcast by the Green Party candidate for the London mayoral election

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bailey worries she is a burden on Mitch and Karen after overhearing their conversation about money, so she tells them the expenses for the space camp are covered for the winners - to the relief of Mitch. But that's just the start of her plan. Linda confronts Nancy about what she found in her coat, and Honey asks Estelle why the other children are being paid more than Janet. Mila finds herself homeless, and Ben and Callum get bad news about the wedding

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Self-made millionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies encounter a Mancunian pair with a dream of sharing music through an app. A husband and wife stand their ground under fierce interrogation over their ethical clothing verification platform, while matters become emotionally charged when an Irish entrepreneur tells the story behind her active shape-wear range - proving even the toughest of Dragons' have a heart. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Four people claim to be an artist called Erica, living with partner Sam in a stylish property in south-east London. Panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix and guest Katie Piper are tasked with determining which is the true Erica, as each of the quartet airs their opinion on the real Erica's taste in fashion, her passion for Sam, the story of their wedding day and the name of their plumber. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: The Covid-19 pandemic reveals the enormity of the challenge that climate change poses, as the drop in emissions during lockdown proves far from enough to get the world back on track. She explores new measures to reduce the level of emissions further, visiting a tree plantation and learning about a machine that sucks carbon dioxide out of the air. She also explains the everyday choices that members of the public can make to reduce their environmental impact

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin visit properties in Peckham, south-east London, Bilston near Wolverhampton, and a flat in Maidstone

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works with one of the UK's biggest water companies. He traces the flow of this precious resource from the reservoir to homes and businesses, before finding out what happens with wastewater at the other end of the system

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Paisley, a young cyclist has his bike ride cut short after a tangle with his chain leaves him with a gash in his leg. He's full of bravado, but badly injured. Nearby, an elderly lady with breathing problems needs to be treated and carefully transferred to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with experts Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper. Before the reds and blues' chosen items go under the hammer, Charles Hanson auctions off a rare Chinese treasure that was discovered inside a carrier bag on its way to a charity shop

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. In Birmingham, a buyer who was born on the weekend of the first moon landings seeks to mark her 50th year with something 'out of this world'

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to Shropshire and Herefordshire to help a recently retired couple with a £550,000 budget find a dream country home that is full of traditional period charm. Nicki also visits the Weston Park Model Air Show

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph is at the Walsall Recycling Centre, where she digs out a bamboo chair uncovered from some garden waste, an unwanted pine corner unit, and a mid-century bedside table

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher is thrilled to find himself working on a remarkable, quirky train set designed and built by celebrated cartoonist and innovator Rowland Emmet, the man behind the inventions in Chitty Chitty Bang Bang. A sailor's trinket box and a doll also receive some much-needed attention

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones rounds off the working week, introducing the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Royal Wedding: A Day to Remember
programme.desc: A look back at the wedding of Prince William and Catherine Middleton, to mark their 10th anniversary. The event was staged at Westminster Abbey on April 29, 2011 and then-Archbishop of Canterbury Rowan Williams conducted the marriage. Around 1,900 people attended the ceremony, which was followed by a luncheon reception at Buckingham Palace

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mitch gets a shock when he goes to wake Bailey. Keegan and Tiffany are nervous ahead of their meeting with the bank manager. Honey threatens Billy that she will cancel Janet's photo shoot if he does not resolve her pay issues with Estelle

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong guest hosts, inviting comedian Joe Lycett and journalist Kirsty Wark to join regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now the main man on BBC magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer. However, conscious of being left in the shadow of co-presenter Jennie and alert to potential changes behind the scenes, he grows ever more anxious about his place in the big time. Return of the media-based comedy, starring Steve Coogan, with Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The second of two compilations of highlights from the recent series, featuring appearances by Tom Cruise, Rebel Wilson, Rege-Jean Page, Rosamund Pike, Tom Jones, Keeley Hawes, Ian Wright, Sienna Miller, Sam Neill, Kate Winslet, Alan Carr, Felicity Kendal, Adrian Lester, Amy Poehler, Daniel Kaluuya, Jennifer Garner, Stanley Tucci, Cush Jumbo, Liam Neeson, Octavia Spencer, Melissa McCarthy and Orlando Bloom

programme.title: The Blair Witch Project (1999)
programme.desc: Three film students head into the American backwoods to make a documentary about the fabled Blair Witch. There proves to be more to the urban legend than meets the eye, and as night falls they find themselves in very real danger. Horror, presented as camcorder footage shot by the characters during their investigation, starring Heather Donahue, Michael Williams and Joshua Leonard

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom finds out what it takes to fill the UK's freezers with peas, joining in with the harvest on a Yorkshire farm and helping at the factory where they are bagged and frozen

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph scours Earlswood Recycling Centre in Surrey for quality items, uncovering a travel trunk, a 1940s utility cabinet and a dressing table with sentimental value

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the North East, Yorkshire and Humber region go head to head and cook their six-course menus again. Guest judge Sophie Conran, cook, author and designer, helps decide the winner. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A Scottish dog lover showcases a range of natural doggie treats, hoping Dragons Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani, Sara Davies and Touker Suleyman will throw her a financial bone. Others braving the heat of the Den include a beauty entrepreneur with her range of affordable-yet-luxury waterless face oils and a Northumbrian inventor hoping to demonstrate his nifty construction bracket. Plus, a tech entrepreneur is put through her paces when she showcases fitness app aimed solely at women. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session day 12 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the quarter-final between Neil Robertson and Kyren Wilson concludes, and Mark Williams v Mark Allen or Mark Selby continues

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session day 12 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where Anthony McGill v Stuart Bingham in the quarter-final concludes, and Shaun Murphy or Yan Bingtao v Judd Trump continues

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to leafy Hatch End in North West London, where he meets the great-nephew of the famous illustrator William Heath Robinson. Michael continues his journey through Slough and Stoke Poges before reaching Windsor, where he surveys the great walls of the castle, chief residence of the British monarch and the oldest and largest occupied castle in the world

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session day 12 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where two quarter-finals conclude - Mark Williams v Mark Allen or Mark Selby, and Shaun Murphy or Yan Bingtao v Judd Trump. The top three seeds all fell in the last eight of 2020, with Judd Trump, Neil Robertson and Williams losing to Kyren Wilson, Selby and Ronnie O'Sullivan respectively

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Paul Cunningham, Phelim O'Hagan, Gemma Austin and Andy Scollick compete for Northern Ireland with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. Given Belfast's renowned connection with the Titanic, there is much luxury maritime inspiration to be drawn, but the chefs also plunder Northern Ireland's other long and varied list of inventions

programme.title: Bent Coppers: Crossing the Line of Duty
programme.desc: The final instalment reveals how, in the late 1970s, corruption within the City of London's CID had grown out of control - and how Operation Countryman was launched in response

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Matt Lucas, Jerry Springer and Cariad Lloyd join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'noodles' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Featuring the conclusion of the quarter-finals - Mark Williams v Mark Allen or Mark Selby, and Shaun Murphy or Yan Bingtao v Judd Trump

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Quarter-final action on day 12 from Sheffield's Crucible Theatre, where the world's elite have gathered

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: Police are called after a wealthy man suspected to be linked to drug crime was taken from his home in central Manchester by an armed gang. After locating the address where the victim was held and tortured, forensic evidence leads officers to name suspects, but while the hunt is on to find these dangerous criminals, another horrific kidnap happens

programme.title: Climate Change: Ade on the Frontline
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan scours the globe for solutions to environmental problems, examining the natural and technological fixes that can help people slow the rate of decline and adapt to shifts already taking place. He begins in the Solomon Islands and then travels down the east coast of Australia from the Great Barrier Reef to Tasmania, learning about the 'feminisation' of green turtles and coming face-to-face with the terror of a bushfire

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray create a bargain border from scratch, Kirsty Wilson creates a recycled birdhouse with its own living roof, and Brian Cunningham and his family make family-friendly, no-dig vegetable plots in the lawn

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets a family who make some of the best Gouda cheese in Britain, and uses their creations to make a tortilla dish with caramelised apples and onions. In the fishing village of Mevagissey, he discovers the origins of the sea shanty and the history of the pilchard fishing industry, when shoals of pilchards were so large they would attract killer whales

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins the East Yorkshire bus company in Hull, getting behind the wheel of a double decker, making sure the vehicles go out on time and helping to keep them in perfect working order

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore explores Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, discovering a set of dining chairs with a matching table, a set of vintage lawn games, and some old aluminium lamp-shades

programme.title: Attenborough's Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the many ways animals use colour, with cameras built specifically for this series revealing a world normally invisible to human eyes. The peacock's tail has evolved entirely to impress females and is one of the most extreme colour ornaments in the natural world, while mandrill baboons signal their strength and status with striking red and blue face masks, the brighter the colours, the higher ranking the male

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day 13 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the first semi-final gets under way, with eight frames scheduled to be played. Anthony McGill dominated this corresponding session in 2020, taking a 6-2 lead over Kyren Wilson in a clash between two players involved in their maiden World Championship last-four match. However, it did not prove to be a decisive lead, with Wilson eventually winning an eventful match 17-16

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo resumes his tour of the Home Counties in the historic town of Guildford. Later, in Chilworth, he follows his 1936 Bradshaw's guide to Newlands Corner on the slopes of the North Downs. Eventually he crosses into Hampshire to reach Aldershot

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening's play on day 13 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the first session of the second semi-final. Eight frames are due to take place tonight, with a further three sessions in total taking place in the match tomorrow and Saturday, as the players continue their bids to win the title claimed by Ronnie O'Sullivan in 2020

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining chefs compete over dishes inspired by science and innovation, drawing especially from Northern Ireland's legacy of inventions. But whose inventions in the kitchen will tough veteran judge, Daniel Clifford score highest? Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: The stories of ordinary people caught up in the biggest events in living memory, and the person who changed their lives. It begins with Karl, a trainee clinical psychologist who was a passenger on the first carriage of the Piccadilly Line London Underground train - one of the four targets of the 7/7 bombings. After the bomb exploded, Karl was comforted by a woman who held his hand in the smoke-filled carriage as he feared for his life. Cameras also follow Emina, an NHS researcher living in Nottingham. She and her younger sister Edina, who was diagnosed with Down Syndrome as a baby, were born in Sarajevo. Edina is searching for the doctor who helped her family evacuate to safety, and gave Edina the chance of a better life. Anita Rani presents

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Eshaan Akbar, Ed Byrne, Sophie Duker and Ed Gamble. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: After entering the local line dancing endurance challenge Mandy finds herself up against an old rival. Comedy, starring Diane Morgan, with Maxine Peake

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's national and international news stories, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day 13 in Sheffield, featuring the opening session of the second semi-final

programme.title: Climate Change: Ade on the Frontline
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan visits Bangladesh's Ganges delta, which is under threat from increasingly extreme weather, and joins a government effort to plant huge new areas with mangroves to defend the low-lying country from tidal surges. He then travels to the kingdom of Bhutan to discover how melting Himalayan glaciers affect the entire world, but also hears from the prime minister how his country has become carbon negative

programme.title: Climate Change: Ade on the Frontline
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan visits Scandinavia, meeting climate change activist Greta Thunberg in Stockholm to find out how the young generation is determined to turn things around. He also learns how the Danish capital Copenhagen has pledged to be carbon neutral by 2025, and has a tour of a Gothenburg house installed with a hydrogen fuel system that could play a massive part in a carbon-free future

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Taking place in a specially built workshop nestled in Birmingham's historic Jewellery Quarter, Katherine Ryan hosts as eight talented jewellers battle it out to prove they can be the next big jewellery-making sensation. In each episode, the competitors are faced with two very different challenges, with their work judged by are two of the biggest names in the business, Solange Azagury-Partridge and Shaun Leane, before one contestants leaves the competition

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The return of the sewing competition in which 12 keen amateurs take part in three challenges, all judged by Savile Row tailor Patrick Grant and fashion designer Esme Young. The sewers start in their efforts to impress with the first pattern challenge, creating the simple, clean lines of the versatile classic sleeveless blouse called a 'shell top', and, for the transformation challenge, they must turn old T-shirts into completely new garments for men, women or children, in just 90 minutes. In the made-to-measure, mannequins are replaced with real-life models, but who will whip up a well-fitting buffet dress and win themselves garment of the week, and whose hopes of staying in the competition will flounder? Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets chef Tom Adams, who runs Combeshead Farm, a leading field-to-fork restaurant where they grow and produce all of their own food, from sourdough bread to pork pies, as well as rearing a small herd of Mangalitsa pigs, a hairy, old-world breed that produces exquisite hams. Rick tries out a new recipe - pork chops with a sloe berry sauce - before moving on to the village of Rock, where his son Jack holds a clam bake on the shore of the Camel Estuary

programme.desc: Dom Littlewood takes a walk on the wild side, working as a zookeeper at the largest zoo in the UK. As he mucks in, he discovers that the zoo's conservation efforts are helping make sure that many of these endangered species survive in the wild for generations to come

programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Woking's Recycling Centre, and singles out a retro swivel chair, a large oak dining table, and a once-cherished sideboard for some restoration work

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day 14 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the first semi-final continues, with eight frames scheduled to take place. Mark Selby was in good form against Ronnie O'Sullivan in last year's corresponding session, winning six frames to take a 9-7 lead in the match. However, it was 'the Rocket' who would eventually win the match and go on to win this title for the sixth time in his career, taking him to joint second on the all-time list

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for Farnborough, Hampshire, famous today for its airshow and home to what was then the Royal Aircraft Establishment. In Basingstoke he visits a glorious neo-Gothic stately home set in the North Wessex Downs

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the evening session on day 14 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the third session of the first semi-final takes place. Both last-four matches were classics last year, with Kyren Wilson and Ronnie O'Sullivan each prevailing 17-16, against Anthony McGill and Mark Selby respectively

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from Northern Ireland go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. They serve the panel of judges, and one of Britain's best known designers, Thomas Heatherwick. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don give a masterclass on growing Mediterranean herbs, plants lily bulbs for summer scent, and in the vegetable garden, he begins to sow seeds for salad root crops

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Nigel Havers, Alex Brooker, Sally Lindsay, Georgia Toffolo and Desiree Burch play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's national and international news stories. With Faisal Islam

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from day 14 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the third session of the opening semi-final

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: With time running out for Starbuck and her crew to find Earth, she decides to run the risk of accepting an offer of truce from Leoben. Meanwhile, Chief Tyrol remains haunted by Callie's death, Gaius broadcasts his preaching via the radio and President Roslin's cancer treatment becomes more intensive

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: In an attempt to discover the way to Earth, Starbuck accompanies Leoben to the Cylon base ship, where the Hybrid issues her with a cryptic warning. Meanwhile, Admiral Adama becomes anxious that the pilot has not yet returned and President Roslin bonds with another cancer patient. Katee Sackhoff, Edward James Olmos and Mary McDonnell star

programme.title: Pandemic 2020
programme.desc: As the pandemic continues, people are increasingly looking for someone to blame, and asking whether life can ever return to normal. Covid-19 deniers become more numerous and vocal, as alternative and conspiratorial thought battles conventional science-based reason

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: When a man is gunned down on a busy residential street in Manchester, a team of specialist GMP detectives race to identify the masked men responsible

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the structure in its 25th anniversary year, following the teams who keep everything on track through the undersea tunnel. Workers in the tunnel paint two giant murals that will link to each other across the sea, and the catenary team head out in their van to look for repairs. Inside the tunnel, the high-speed trains are being slowed down by the air they push in front of them, and the only way to relieve the pressure is with tubes connecting one tunnel to another

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross leads viewers into the beauty of the dry lands as he creates an oval painting for beginners and accomplished artists as well

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the evening session day 12 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where two quarter-finals conclude - Mark Williams v Mark Allen or Mark Selby, and Shaun Murphy or Yan Bingtao v Judd Trump. The top three seeds all fell in the last eight of 2020, with Trump, Neil Robertson and Williams falling to Kyren Wilson, Selby and Ronnie O'Sullivan respectively

programme.title: Watergate
programme.desc: A focus on the so-called Saturday Night Massacre, the chain of events that unfolded on October 20, 1973 when President Nixon demanded that special prosecutor Archibald Cox be fired

programme.title: Watergate
programme.desc: The documentary examines how mounting evidence against Richard Nixon led to his resignation. After compelling evidence was found on the automatic taping system in the Oval Office, the House voted for impeachment and the president resigned

programme.title: Handmade
programme.desc: Filmed in real time and without voiceover or music, this series examines the crafting of handmade objects. The first programme observes expert Michael Ruh carrying out the time-consuming process of blowing and rolling glass to make a simple jug. Glass is heated in a steel crucible until it becomes liquid. Ruh's task is to keep a globule hot and in constant motion as he shapes it

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross leads viewers into the beauty of the dry lands as he creates an oval painting for beginners and accomplished artists as well

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the structure in its 25th anniversary year, following the teams who keep everything on track through the undersea tunnel. Workers in the tunnel paint two giant murals that will link to each other across the sea, and the catenary team head out in their van to look for repairs. Inside the tunnel, the high-speed trains are being slowed down by the air they push in front of them, and the only way to relieve the pressure is with tubes connecting one tunnel to another

programme.title: Lights Up
programme.desc: A new production of The Winter's Tale by the Royal Shakespeare Company, presented as being set between 1953 and 1969 - the years of the Coronation and the moon landing. King Leontes rips his family apart with his jealousy, but grief opens his heart. Starring Joseph Kloska and Kemi-Bo Jacobs, and directed by RSC Deputy Artistic Director Erica Whyman

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: Twenty-five years since the Channel Tunnel was opened, many staff face retirement and new recruits are being drafted in. But the tests for wannabe shuttle drivers are ruthless, with a 90 per cent fail rate. Meanwhile, supercars turn up in convoy for the Le Mans 24-hour race. But as the French step up their passport controls, and the screens break down at terminal control, there are concerns that the vehicles will not make it to France in time

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross captures a spectacular view in a painting that offers a lesson in creating rugged and rocky natural surfaces

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the evening's play on day 13 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the first session of the second semi-final. Eight frames are due to take place tonight, with a further three sessions in total taking place in the match tomorrow and Saturday, as the players continue their bids to win the title claimed by Ronnie O'Sullivan in 2020

programme.title: Being AP (2015)
programme.desc: Documentary about jockey AP McCoy, who has been crowned champion jockey 19 years running. The film catches him at a turning point in his sporting career, as the desire to achieve a record-breaking run of victories and the pressure to retire from horse racing, while his wife fears the increasingly challenging nature of the sport puts him at risk of serious injury

programme.title: Great War Horses
programme.desc: An account of the men and horses of the Australian Light Horse regiments, and the pivotal role they played in the First World War at the Battle of Romani in 1916, the Light Horse charge at the Battle of Beersheba in 1917, and the capture of Damascus in 1918. Bred for Australia's tough Outback conditions, the horses, known as Walers, were well equipped for the harsh climate and terrain of the Middle East, where the Anzac forces faced the armies of the Ottoman Empire

programme.title: A History of Art in Three Colours
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox turns his attention to blue, examining how the colour was originally created from lapis lazuli, an imported precious stone found in the East, and that the bright paint it produced captivated artists in the Middle Ages. He explores the renowned Florentine painter Giotto's ground-breaking religious frescoes in the Arena Chapel in Padua, and charts how the colour has also helped painters from more recent times, including Gaugin, Van Gogh and Picasso, to depict emotional depth

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross captures a spectacular view in a painting that offers a lesson in creating rugged and rocky natural surfaces

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: Twenty-five years since the Channel Tunnel was opened, many staff face retirement and new recruits are being drafted in. But the tests for wannabe shuttle drivers are ruthless, with a 90 per cent fail rate. Meanwhile, supercars turn up in convoy for the Le Mans 24-hour race. But as the French step up their passport controls, and the screens break down at terminal control, there are concerns that the vehicles will not make it to France in time

programme.title: Fighting for King and Empire: Britain's Caribbean Heroes
programme.desc: During the Second World War, thousands of men and women from Britain's Caribbean colonies volunteered to join the fight against Hitler, but their contribution has largely been forgotten. In this documentary based on the film Divided By Race - United in War and Peace, veterans tell their extraordinary wartime stories and reveal how they have faced a lifelong struggle to be treated as equals by the UK government and the British people

programme.title: BBC Young Musician
programme.desc: Extensive highlights of the semi-final, which was filmed just days before the first UK-wide lockdown back in March last year. The semi-final sees the winners of the keyboard, strings, brass, woodwind and percussion finals compete for the three coveted places in the grand final. Organist and conductor Anna Lapwood is joined by Tim Rhys-Evans - director of music at the Royal Welsh College of Music and Drama who provides insight and analysis. Josie D'Arby also joins the line-up with interviews with the semi-finalists and Jess Gillam continues her series of conversations discussing the changing face of classical music with conductor Nicholas Collon

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the evening session on day 14 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the third session of the first semi-final takes place. Both last-four matches were classics last year, with Kyren Wilson and Ronnie O'Sullivan each prevailing 17-16, against Anthony McGill and Mark Selby respectively

programme.title: Ronnie's: Ronnie Scott & His World-Famous Jazz Club
programme.desc: The history of Ronnie Scott's Jazz Club, eponymously named after the late tenor saxophonist who founded the establishment with business partner Pete King. It opened in London's Soho in October 1959, inspired by the vibrant post-war jazz venues in New York. Since then, Ronnie's has consistently played host to many of the world's greatest jazz legends, including Chet Baker, Count Basie, Miles Davies, Ella Fitzgerald, Buddy Rich and Nina Simone who are all featured in the film. Contributors include Ronnie's friends, family and the music elite, including Chris Blackwell, Quincy Jones and Sonny Rollins

programme.title: Jazz 625 Live: For One Night Only
programme.desc: A one-off revival of the iconic 1960s music show. Broadcasting live from Cheltenham during the Jazz Festival, the special show will feature a house band - led by pianist Robert Mitchell - with special guests including Gregory Porter, Joshua Redman, Jacqui Dankworth and Cleo Laine. Plus, an exclusive recorded performance from Rolling Stones drummer Charlie Watts in a jazz quartet with saxophonist Scott Hamilton. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Musicians Midge Ure and Kim Appleby visit London and Manchester, the two cities that did battle with each other for musical pre-eminence as '80s music turned towards the new sounds of dance. This is a tale of how studio technology changed music - with British bands putting their own unique spin on dance to produce contrasting northern and southern sounds. Contributors include Denise Pearson from Five Star, Soul II Soul's Jazzie B, Mark Moore of S-Express, Shaun Ryder from the Happy Mondays and Peter Hook of New Order

programme.title: Jazz Divas Gold
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances by acclaimed female jazz artists from shows including Not So Much a Programme - More a Way of Life, Morecambe and Wise, Parkinson and Show of the Week. Featuring Ella Fitzgerald, Peggy Lee, Blossom Dearie, Nina Simone, Cleo Laine, Marion Montgomery, Betty Carter, Sarah Vaughan, Eartha Kitt and Amy Winehouse

programme.desc: It's Frosty Frolics Day, and Love Monster plans to spend it with Bad Idea Puppy and his cousin Good Idea Puppy, who has come to visit. Love Monster thinks that since Good Idea Puppy is the guest, she should choose which activity they do first. She decides that they should start with the ice slide. Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won't be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren't fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.

programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome little ones Oakley, Arthur, Luna, Reau, Ty’lana and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a toy camera, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are themed around a day out. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate toys and objects inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The fun song Looking Out on Our Day Out features, as does a story involving Baby Bear. Viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring things that are themed around a day out too. The Together Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie is having a party! She finds out how bubbles work when we blow into bubble mixture. She shows us how the surface tension of water changes when we add soap and how we can stop bubbles from bursting with our hands, and using her slow-motion camera, she shows how bubbles make a sphere shape. Next, Maddie heads to a factory where a party favourite is made - chocolate! We learn how chocolate starts out as a fruit, and we see the chocolates being made for an assortment box. programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: A half-asleep Love Monster exits his house and walks towards the green, passing snoozing citizens on the way. He notices that the whole of the green is covered with litter and snoring Fluffytowners. Love Monster remembers that yesterday was Woo Hoo Day and the whole of Fluffytown had a big party - which makes today the Day After Woo Hoo Day. Love Monster attempts to clean up all of the mess on his own as a nice surprise for his friends, but he struggles and soon realises that it is a very big job. He should ask for help! He wakes everyone up, saying if everyone picks up one piece of litter each, they can tidy up the green in no time. Elder Kitten says he knows just the song for the job. Love Monster and Elder Kitten lead the Fluffytowners in singing a funky, tidying up song as they each pick up a piece of litter to clean the green. Everyone takes their piece of litter to the recycling centre where they queue up to do their bit for the environment.

programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome little ones Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, River, Araiya and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag are numbered building blocks, and all the parents and little ones have different objects and toys that are themed around numbers. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate number-themed objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The traditional children’s nursery rhyme One, Two, Buckle My Shoe features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring numbers too. The Action Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

Meanwhile, Jemima goes to a play cafe, where she enjoys a tea party with mum and dad. Back at home, Jac and dad plant their new greenery and talk about how it’ll grow big and tall, just like Jac! Jemima finishes the day with painting flowers using her hands, but she must remember to wash them afterwards! Jac has his very first haircut - what will he think? programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie is relaxing at home, playing games and watching television. She shows us how a television screen works using millions of tiny squares called pixels. Using her special microscope camera, Maddie shows us how a pixel can make any colour from just three coloured lights - red, blue and green. Next she visits a factory where carpet is made. Maddie follows the journey that wool takes, from shearing a sheep to going through the factory, to end up in the carpets we have at home. Maddie uses her special slow-motion camera to show us how the different coloured threads are woven together on a loom to make a carpet.

Plus, Will Kirk takes on the repair of a 3D wooden boat puzzle

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook travels to Gower to meet and eat with a little-known community of incomers enjoying life on one of the country's most beautiful coastlines. At the heart of the community are the Blytt-Jordens - three generations of Welsh-Norwegians who are among several such clans settled in the area who have only recently discovered each other. In Michela's company, they rustle up traditional Norwegian delicacies from national pudding tilsorte bondepiker to hot waffles and brown cheese and fermented fish. They also get a chance to chat in their first language

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the experts get to work on more heirlooms to bring them, and the memories they hold, back to life. Dominic Chinea faces the heavy-duty task of repairing a seized-up retro roundabout that reminds its owner Stella of her three daughters' childhoods. Wood expert Will Kirk is amazed when a First World War dominoes set, thought to have been played in the Christmas truce of 1914, arrives in the barn. Suzie Fletcher uses her leather skills on a 19th-century women's side saddle bought in Texas, and Julie and Amanda need the help of mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher to fix a broken teddy bear

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's gentlemen's classics week, and the sewers' skills are tested to the limit as they tackle headwear - a first for the show - in the shape of a Peaky Blinders-style baker boy cap, before being given 90 minutes to smarten up and transform second-hand men's jackets and blazers into a garment for a woman. In the made-to-measure challenge, they must create a casual utility jacket, with a relaxed fit and at least three pockets. Joe Lycett follows their progress

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts presents the weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: Is Uni Racist?
programme.desc: Britain's universities are some of the most prestigious in the world, but are they safe places for students of colour? Linda Adey investigates the experiences of black and ethnic minority students and examines what happens when victims of racist abuse at university want to complain

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ronan Keating, as they assist a father and son from Crawley in picking out the best singers - and get a few dance lessons in return

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood takes a walk on the wild side, working as a zookeeper at the largest zoo in the UK. As he mucks in, he discovers that the zoo's conservation efforts are helping make sure that many of these endangered species survive in the wild for generations to come

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations paramedic Brian is called to help his colleagues at a bus crash in Glasgow that has left passengers injured. programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts as experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items. The presenter also receives a visit by the chairman of the Torquay Pottery Collectors Society, who has a rather special pasty to show him

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: When a proud mother faces her own mortality, she opens up a can of worms that threatens to destroy her relationship with her daughter. Valerie is delighted when Ricky Delaine propositions her, while Rob and Harriet start to bond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Eighteen contestants remain in the running to win £10,000, including Praveen, Chris, Melanie and Gareth - aware that a wrong answer at any stage will see them knocked out

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley showcases an arresting range of houses to a couple who have a substantial budget of £700,000 and are hoping to relocate their family to the Norfolk countryside. For over half a century, Norfolk has been home to Lotus cars, so while in the county Ginny finds out more about the company heritage before catching a glimpse of the future when their latest hypercar is unveiled

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore, upholsterer Simion Hawtin-Smith and print artist Zoe Murphy breathe new life into a retro swivel chair, a large oak dining table and a once-cherished sideboard

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Radio expert Mark Stuckey has a job on his hands, repairing a 1930s transistor radio brought into the Repair Shop by David Hill from Brecon. The radio was given to David's father by an army officer on the beaches at Dunkirk during the Second World War, and after being successfully shepherded through the bullets and the crashing waves to safety, it became the Hill family's pride and joy. However, the subsequent years have not been kind, with the vintage transistor battered and broken both inside and out. A battered leather gaming case and a grandfather clock with a poignant history are also given a new lease of life

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: The Covid-19 pandemic reveals the enormity of the challenge that climate change poses, as the drop in emissions during lockdown proves far from enough to get the world back on track. She explores new measures to reduce the level of emissions further, visiting a tree plantation and learning about a machine that sucks carbon dioxide out of the air. She also explains the everyday choices that members of the public can make to reduce their environmental impact

programme.title: Royal Wedding: A Day to Remember
programme.desc: A look back at the wedding of Prince William and Catherine Middleton, to mark their 10th anniversary. The event was staged at Westminster Abbey on April 29, 2011 and then-Archbishop of Canterbury Rowan Williams conducted the marriage. Around 1,900 people attended the ceremony, which was followed by a luncheon reception at Buckingham Palace

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mitch gets a shock when he goes to wake Bailey. Keegan and Tiffany are nervous ahead of their meeting with the bank manager. Honey threatens Billy that she will cancel Janet's photo shoot if he does not resolve her pay issues with Estelle

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong guest hosts, inviting comedian Joe Lycett and journalist Kirsty Wark to join regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now the main man on BBC magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer. However, conscious of being left in the shadow of co-presenter Jennie and alert to potential changes behind the scenes, he grows ever more anxious about his place in the big time. Return of the media-based comedy, starring Steve Coogan, with Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: A celebration of the entrepreneurial spirit among the young. This opening episode features Nanial Jones, who invested all his savings into a vintage clothing business. Plus, Alaw Haf who now makes £5,000 a month from subscriptions on the OnlyFans website, having lost her gym job in lockdown, and Toni Leigh Hall who has launched a new line of humorous cards, calendars, door-mats, mouse-mats and face masks

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The second of two compilations of highlights from the recent series, featuring appearances by Tom Cruise, Rebel Wilson, Rege-Jean Page, Rosamund Pike, Tom Jones, Keeley Hawes, Ian Wright, Sienna Miller, Sam Neill, Kate Winslet, Alan Carr, Felicity Kendal, Adrian Lester, Amy Poehler, Daniel Kaluuya, Jennifer Garner, Stanley Tucci, Cush Jumbo, Liam Neeson, Octavia Spencer, Melissa McCarthy and Orlando Bloom

programme.title: The Blair Witch Project (1999)
programme.desc: Three film students head into the American backwoods to make a documentary about the fabled Blair Witch. There proves to be more to the urban legend than meets the eye, and as night falls they find themselves in very real danger. Horror, presented as camcorder footage shot by the characters during their investigation, starring Heather Donahue, Michael Williams and Joshua Leonard

programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple of keen horticulturists who have £450,000 to find a home in the Yorkshire Dales, and also visits RHS Garden Harlow Carr to meet Alan Titchmarsh

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore explores Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, discovering a set of dining chairs with a matching table, a set of vintage lawn games, and some old aluminium lamp-shades

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea takes on the restoration of a once brightly coloured wooden carousel horse, while ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay is charged with the task of repairing a little cat teapot with an extraordinary wartime story behind it. Plus, Will Kirk takes on the repair of a 3D wooden boat puzzle

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Scottish Liberal Democrats

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Dylan takes Ruby out on a date and works up the courage to tell her how he feels, while Rebecca's lies finally catchup with her, an anxious Lou prepares for her interview, and Alex has a difficult decision to make

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Kathy from Leicester presents the dealers with a catalogue for an auction to help people who lost loved ones in the Titanic disaster. Alice from Wales presents then with an unusual moulded men's underwear advert from the early 20th century - although the dealers are not so sure when they realise a bit is missing - and James from Stoke-on-Trent hopes to sell a pair of Victorian horse hair singers. Presented by Nigel Havers, with expert Simon Bower

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the experts get to work on more heirlooms to bring them, and the memories they hold, back to life. Dominic Chinea faces the heavy-duty task of repairing a seized-up retro roundabout that reminds its owner Stella of her three daughters' childhoods. Wood expert Will Kirk is amazed when a First World War dominoes set, thought to have been played in the Christmas truce of 1914, arrives in the barn. Suzie Fletcher uses her leather skills on a 19th-century women's side saddle bought in Texas, and Julie and Amanda need the help of mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher to fix a broken teddy bear

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's gentlemen's classics week, and the sewers' skills are tested to the limit as they tackle headwear - a first for the show - in the shape of a Peaky Blinders-style baker boy cap, before being given 90 minutes to smarten up and transform second-hand men's jackets and blazers into a garment for a woman. In the made-to-measure challenge, they must create a casual utility jacket, with a relaxed fit and at least three pockets. Joe Lycett follows their progress

programme.title: Is Uni Racist?
programme.desc: Britain's universities are some of the most prestigious in the world, but are they safe places for students of colour? Linda Adey investigates the experiences of black and ethnic minority students and examines what happens when victims of racist abuse at university want to complain

programme.title: Scottish Questions
programme.desc: Ministers are put on the spot by fellow MPs in their monthly question-and-answer session at Westminster

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: The best of the week's entertainment news, with Amy Irons and David Farrell

programme.title: Debate Night
programme.desc: Topical debate on the big issues affecting Scotland and beyond. Members of the public have an hour to put their questions to a panel of politicians and other public figures about the biggest stories and the things they care about the most

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Wolverhampton to see a three-bed, end-of-terrace house with scope to extend. He's also in Willenhall in the West Midlands to visit a bungalow which might offer more potential as a new building plot. In Norfolk, Tommy Walsh visits a three-bed house that he's a big fan of

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood takes a walk on the wild side, working as a zookeeper at the largest zoo in the UK. As he mucks in, he discovers that the zoo's conservation efforts are helping make sure that many of these endangered species survive in the wild for generations to come

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations paramedic Brian is called to help his colleagues at a bus crash in Glasgow that has left passengers injured. Near Peterhead, an elderly patient who has difficulty moving, needs to be transferred to hospital, so ambulance crew members Ricky and Luan use their skills to help him into the vehicle

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts as experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items. The presenter also receives a visit by the chairman of the Torquay Pottery Collectors Society, who has a rather special pasty to show him

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: When a proud mother faces her own mortality, she opens up a can of worms that threatens to destroy her relationship with her daughter. Valerie is delighted when Ricky Delaine propositions her, while Rob and Harriet start to bond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Eighteen contestants remain in the running to win £10,000, including Praveen, Chris, Melanie and Gareth - aware that a wrong answer at any stage will see them knocked out

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley showcases an arresting range of houses to a couple who have a substantial budget of £700,000 and are hoping to relocate their family to the Norfolk countryside. For over half a century, Norfolk has been home to Lotus cars, so while in the county Ginny finds out more about the company heritage before catching a glimpse of the future when their latest hypercar is unveiled

programme.desc: Sarah Moore, upholsterer Simion Hawtin-Smith and print artist Zoe Murphy breathe new life into a retro swivel chair, a large oak dining table and a once-cherished sideboard

Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Four people claim to be an artist called Erica, living with partner Sam in a stylish property in south-east London. Panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix and guest Katie Piper are tasked with determining which is the true Erica, as each of the quartet airs their opinion on the real Erica's taste in fashion, her passion for Sam, the story of their wedding day and the name of their plumber. Presented by Stacey Dooley
