programme.desc: When a famous author receives death threats, Peralta and Jeffords take the case so that Jeffords can meet his lifelong hero. Gina wants to choose the location of the annual Boyle family vacation, and Santiago gets in the middle of a fight between Captain Holt and Kevin

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny tries to help Sheldon when he starts to wonder whether he is wasting his time studying string theory. Meanwhile, during a dinner, Raj discovers Howard once embarrassed himself on a date with Emily. Stephen Hawking guest stars

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny and Leonard try to distract Sheldon from his career woes by reinstating Anything Can Happen Thursday, but it causes friction among the gang when Amy and Bernadette are caught lying. Meanwhile, Raj seeks Howard's help in preparing for a date with Emily. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox USA
programme.desc: A rolling cast of famous faces in the US critique the week's TV, including Ultimate Tag, 2020 Beverly Hills Dog Show and Deadliest Catch

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang goes to a bar and runs into Penny's old boyfriend Zack, while Bernadette is left to spend the evening with Sheldon when she cannot go wine tasting with the others. Elsewhere, things do not go to plan when Raj introduces his new girlfriend, Claire. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Inbetweeners Movie (2011)
programme.desc: Awkward teenagers Simon, Will, Jay and Neil plan a holiday to the Greek island of Crete after they finish their exams, hoping to experience drink, sex and debauchery beyond their wildest dreams. However, things do not quite work out as planned. Comedy based on the TV series, with Joe Thomas, Simon Bird, James Buckley and Blake Harrison

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Sheldon discover their favourite childhood TV science show host Professor Proton can be booked for private parties and decide to hire him to entertain them. Meanwhile, Raj asks Howard and Bernadette to look after his dog for the weekend, but the couple panic when the canine goes missing. Guest starring Bob Newhart

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The guys invite the girls to a Dungeons and Dragons night when their trip to Vegas falls through after an incident with airport security. However, following a few drinks, Sheldon and Amy's relationship takes an unexpected turn. Meanwhile, Raj goes on a date with Lucy, who tries to sneak out of the restaurant's bathroom window - but ends up getting stuck and calling for his help

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Twenty-year-old estate agent Katie from Bristol is a self-confessed disaster when it comes to dating, so she hopes Naked Attraction will turn her luck around. London student James, 22, loves clubbing and has a penchant for heels and glitter but he seems to be scaring off the guys. Will any of the men hidden away behind the screens be open-minded enough for him? Anna Richardson reveals all

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson meets 24-year-old fitness fanatic Rose, whose body has been getting all the attention in the gym while her love life has been neglected. She is bored of being one of the lads and hopes to be seen for all that she is. Next up is Clarissa, a 22-year-old barista from Basingstoke who is looking for her dream woman. Will either of tonight's singletons pick the right date when everything is stripped back to basics?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, including Strictly Come Dancing, Lost and Found and I'm a Celebrity, Blue Planet II, Avatar, The Sex Robots Are Coming and the news. From December 2017

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Another set of apparently meaningless tasks are handed to five comedians by Taskmaster Greg Davies and his genial companion Alex Horne. Jessica Knappett piles shoes on some butcher's scales, James Acaster tries the emergency alarm on a lift, and Rhod Gilbert smears mustard in his eyes

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is distraught when his online gaming account is hacked, so his friends decide to find out who is responsible. However, they make the disastrous mistake of confronting the culprit in person. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy and Sheldon start a gossip experiment to see how quickly rumours spread among the gang, while Howard tells his friends he is planning to propose to Bernadette - not realising Raj is wrestling with his own feelings for her. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Mayim Bialik and Johnny Galecki

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: When Adam takes his children to work with him for the day, they accidentally give his biggest client lice. Later, Andi feels like a bad mother when she's the only one in the family not to get lice. Comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc and Liza Snyder

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam notices his dad's increasingly forgetful behaviour, but struggles with how to approach him to discuss it Comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Bonnie's friendly foe Charlotte helps her prepare for the wedding - but fears arise she may have other plans up her sleeve. Comedy, guest starring Reba McEntire, with Melissa Peterman and Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enlists the help of paper-cutting expert Poppy Chancellor to make personalised wedding invitations for a couple about to tie the knot. Plus, the inventive presenter creates a craft collage to celebrate the arrival of a newborn baby

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: When her partner of 20 years died, Ginny Lunn decided to find a holiday home where she could create new memories. Torn between buying a place in Dorset within easy reach of her London home for a budget of £250,000, and the Costa Tropical in Spain, where she would have a budget of £150,000, she requires help. Jasmine Harman shows her three options in Dorset, and Laura Hamilton tries to impress her with three properties in Spain, but, with the help of her sister Sara, which one will Ginny choose?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien restore eight items, including a pine blanket chest, a pair of battlefield telephones and some gothic church windows

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Wales, where they pick up and restore a dentist's table and a barber's chair, and make a coffee table out of parquet flooring

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The researchers are asked to track down the relatives of a Lancashire farmer who died without leaving a will. Plus, the story of a search for heirs that involved two generations of women who had played invaluable roles on Britain's production lines

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Two French B&B hosts receive their first customer review. One expat fisherman has a big catch in Cape Verde, while another comes back empty-handed in Spain, and a boutique Spanish hotel launches a wedding business

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Dunsandles Guest House in New Brighton, Merseyside, where owner Alan Johnson and his professional sidekick Dave Owen hope to win the title - but a scandal with some unwanted hair on the bed sheets is not a good start, and two guests feel they are making an exhibition of themselves with a tea-trolley in full view of the neighbours

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the second day of the competition, the contestants visit the Lady Teal Hotel Boat on the waterways near Burnley in Lancashire, and owners Nick and Gina Mead hope to impress them with an afternoon of entertainment out on the locks. At breakfast, one of the guests admits to not enjoying his stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: From York House in Tetbury, Gloucestershire, where Brock Bergius hopes that his cheeky sense of humour and perfectionist nature will help him win the competition. He is in his element making saucy jokes during an afternoon of sausage making, but his reluctance to stray from the menu at breakfast leaves some of his guests disappointed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Heyford House in Oxfordshire, where Leo and Sonya Brooke-Little hope to impress the contestants with their hospitality. The afternoon event is boxing, which Brock Burgius sees as the perfect opportunity to settle some scores with his fellow competitors

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's winner is announced as the B&B owners meet for the last time to thrash out their differences. Feedback includes criticism of a badly positioned tea tray, controversy over a hot chocolate and accusations of overenthusiastic hosting

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives visit a stately home in Warwickshire as they try to find relatives of a lady who was born there, and an heir on another case embarks on a journey of discovery to learn about the grandfather he never met. Plus, details of unclaimed estates

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The keepers try to work out why male red panda Jung is losing weight, with a full medical examination scheduled after a special diet fails to solve the problem. West African mandrill JC is being challenged for his dominant position by his sons Ludo and Jareth and, while JC is taken for some health checks, Ludo gets 'over-friendly' with his dad's girlfriend. Tempers start to fray among Chester Zoo's Humboldt penguin colony during the annual moult - can youngsters Frazzle and Munch avoid being picked on by the grown-ups?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Belinda travels to Dick and Angel's chateau so that Angel can teach her the art of decoupage on a classic 1950s family dressing table. It's changeover day at Krys and Tim's chateau, as they sweat it out in 42-degree heat to get their 20-room chateau ready for their next booking. Budding chateau owners Elaine and David visit a 19th-century chateau with 18 rooms and two gîtes in Normandy

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits a home built on an airfield in Strathaven in Scotland to check on the progress made by Colin MacKinnon and his partner Marta Briongos. Their ambitious plan was to build a metal-sculptural home inspired by aircraft hangars right next to the runway, but the difficulties of building their elegant design saw a year-long project stretch to three years

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps more people as they build affordable dream homes. He begins in Hertfordshire with retired firefighter Bob and his wife Wendy, who spent years looking for the perfect building plot - until they found it in their back garden. They want somewhere to play music, paint and write, but they soon find themselves facing an ever-expanding budget to finish the house

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison follows more individuals Down Under as they design and construct a new home. He begins with Rob and Sally, who have bought the land for their new house in Mystery Bay, New South Wales, but soon face endless challenges - the building must withstand the elements, celebrate the amazing views and blend effortlessly into the surroundings.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Michelle, who's 54, is airlifted to St George's after being involved in a car crash with her husband Paul in Surrey. She was given a blood transfusion at the scene and doctors work quickly to get her to CT to ascertain the full extent of her injuries. Cameras also follow Derren, 41, who has fractured his ankle while trampolining with his 12-year-old daughter, Macie

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as Sean Lock and Michelle Wolf take on Jon Richardson and Rob Beckett. Susie Dent is joined by Spencer Jones in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps more people as they build affordable dream homes. He begins in Hertfordshire with retired firefighter Bob and his wife Wendy, who spent years looking for the perfect building plot - until they found it in their back garden. They want somewhere to play music, paint and write, but they soon find themselves facing an ever-expanding budget to finish the house

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison follows more individuals Down Under as they design and construct a new home. He begins with Rob and Sally, who have bought the land for their new house in Mystery Bay, New South Wales, but soon face endless challenges - the building must withstand the elements, celebrate the amazing views and blend effortlessly into the surroundings.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team members select some of their favourite investigations from the show. Putting plums, prunes and prune juice to the test, Jimmy Doherty heads to King's College London to make a concoction that will help keep people's bowel movements regular. Kate Quilton visits Vietnam to learn about the difference between black and white pepper, and discovers the remarkable lengths to which producers go to guard their valuable crop from thieves, while Matt Tebbutt is in Switzerland to find out why there are holes in Swiss cheese

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits the world's largest jelly bean producers to discover what they are made of instead of gelatine, and Matt Tebbutt finds out why maple syrup is so expensive

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Juliette and Jonathan Cottam are selling their London home to retire elsewhere, but are torn between two very different locations. Juliette dreams of a life in France, where she spent much of her childhood. With a budget of £250,000, she thinks the Dordogne region would be the perfect location. Jonathan wants to stay in the UK with a budget of up to £350,000 and move to Devon where he grew up. Jasmine Harman tempts Juliette with three properties in Devon, while Lara Hamilton shows them three fantastic French homes

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel around Britain to find items to fix up and flog on to make a profit for their owners. In the Cotswolds, the duo compete to see who can create the most profit on two old lamps and take a spin in a Devonshire fire engine

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel around Britain to find items to fix up and flog on to make a profit for their owners. In this episode, Henry finds a treat for restorer Guy, and Simon creates stunning table lamps from old theatre lights

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives try to find the relatives of a deceased Sidcup woman after she left behind a property in south-east London, but no will. Plus, the hunt for a former political correspondent's family reveals the story of a talented man who enjoyed an illustrious career

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Boutique hotel owners Ray and Yvonne hold their first wedding, while a couple of ex-actors have plans to bring drama to Bordeaux, although a new baby may scupper their dream

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Anglesey, where best pals Richard Tuke and Susan Hatch hope to show that Blackthorn Farm B&B, which they own, is better than any of their guests' establishments. However, they find that their close friendship is scrutinised even more than their property

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The White Swan Hotel in Yeadon, West Yorkshire, is the second stop of the competition, and owners Graham and Barbara Yellow hope their basic facilities and affordable prices will please their guests. Spiritual couple Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor get good vibes from their room, Rachel Lonergan views the hosts in a new light and breakfast is well received by the contestants, despite having to pay a supplement fee

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Vegetarians Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor host at the Old Mill House B&B in Bridport, Dorset, and both come under attack at dinner when Malcolm voices his opinions on meat eating. In the morning, a meditation session gets a mixed response, and most of the guests are left cold by the vegetarian buffet breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Rachel and Dan Lonergan welcome their guests to the Brighton Inn, East Sussex, where they hope to offer a luxury, boutique experience. Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor give their room a thorough inspection, before the groups learns the art of building a human pyramid in the afternoon's activity. At breakfast, they discover a lack of vegetarian alternatives, and the hosts suspect game-playing when they are shocked by some negative feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together to confront their rivals and find out how much they have been paid. Richard and Susan are keen to tackle the bathroom issues of Barbara and Graham, who bask in the comments from Malcolm and Fiona. The Old Mill House proprietors defend their vegetarian breakfast routine, but serve up snobby feedback to Rachel and Dan from the Brighton Inn

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives receive a case from America with a strong showbusiness connection, and must trace the relatives of a woman who left the UK as a child in 1936. Plus, the story of a man adopted as a boy leads to an exotic tale of life on the island of Bermuda

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The keepers are concerned about a three-month-old Andean bear cub, worrying that its mother's overprotective behaviour may curb its development. The pygmy marmosets have new babies and mum Audrey has to carry her twins everywhere she goes to ensure their safety, while young elephant Nandita is desperate to make friends with babies Aayu and Indali, but her boisterous behaviour is proving too much for them - can she learn the maternal skills she needs to win them round and persuade them to go swimming with her?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick and Angel invite Tim and Krys over to their place as Dick shows Tim how to put on a gourmet weekend, and Angel has a heart-to-heart chat about chateau-living with Krys. Lee wrestles with a curtain in the shower room on arrival day for his and Belinda's first paying guests. Plus, Angela and Steve invite eight friends over to test-run their tandem holiday business

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Spanish architect Jaime and his wife Mimi as they embark on a mission to convert a Grade II listed folly into a family home in Buckinghamshire. Giving themselves a wildly optimistic six-month deadline to finish before the birth of their second child, it's not long before the couple hit trouble. Jaime puts all his energies and creativity into the project, using innovative 3D mapping to squeeze living spaces into the tower, but it proves to be an exhausting struggle

programme.title: The Good Fight
programme.desc: Reddick, Boseman & Lockhart is hired to investigate the death of high-profile New York inmate Jeffrey Epstein. Back at the office, STR Laurie continues to tighten its grip on the firm by forcing Diane, Adrian and Liz to conduct layoffs. However, the enraged trio won't go down that route without a fight and hatch a plan in hopes of shaking off their corporate overlords once and for all. Legal drama, starring Christine Baranski, Cush Jumbo and Delroy Lindo

programme.title: Secret Bridesmaids' Business
programme.desc: Drama, starring Abbie Cornish, Katie McGrath and Georgina Haig as three friends brought closer together by an unexpected wedding proposal. However, as preparations for the big day get underway, their bond is tested by a series of shocking revelations and a dangerous obsession that threatens to spiral out of control

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Aaron, 31, is rushed to St George's after overturning his car onto metal railings during a high-speed collision. He has a serious laceration to his face where a spiked railing narrowly missed his eye socket. Cameras also follow Fiona, 65, who is rushed to the hospital after falling over in a restaurant and cutting her knee during her friend Evelyn's 70th birthday party

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Miles Jupp take on Jon Richardson and Rose Matafeo, while Vic Reeves joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Max searches for a solution to crowdfunding after his eye-opening appearance in a viral Go Fund Me video. Reynolds takes his last laps at the hospital, while Kapoor helps a former pilot deal with his life as a quadriplegic. Medical drama, starring Ryan Eggold

programme.title: The Good Fight
programme.desc: Reddick, Boseman & Lockhart is hired to investigate the death of high-profile New York inmate Jeffrey Epstein. Back at the office, STR Laurie continues to tighten its grip on the firm by forcing Diane, Adrian and Liz to conduct layoffs. However, the enraged trio won't go down that route without a fight and hatch a plan in hopes of shaking off their corporate overlords once and for all. Legal drama, starring Christine Baranski, Cush Jumbo and Delroy Lindo

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another selection of the team's favourite investigations. Kate Quilton learns how a special enzyme contained in pineapples attacks and destroys dead skin cells in the mouth, with scientists using this enzyme to treat the victims of burns. Jimmy Doherty investigates the catastrophic effects of not getting enough vitamin D when he meets a boy who has rickets, while Matt Tebbutt finds out how a Lincolnshire business produces 1.5 million bottles of elderflower cordial without employing a single flower-picker

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Mauritius to find out what the difference is between white and dark rum, and learns the explosive origins of the term proof

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Victor and Alison Dennis plan to wind up their IT business and run a B&B in their retirement. They lived in Paris for five years when their children were young, and now Victor wants to move to sunny southern France. However, keen beekeeper Alison would prefer to start a new life in Cornwall. Jasmine Harman helps the pair search for properties in both locations

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo head to Wales to see how can create the most profit on items, including an oil dispenser, fire grate, chaise longue and even a catamaran

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien attempt to restore items including an old cheese press, and a 1980s motorbike almost gets the better of Henry and his restorer Guy

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The team looks for relatives of a woman who was part of the famous Second World War code-breaking mission at Bletchley Park in Buckinghamshire - and believe she may have had a sister. Another case uncovers the rousing story of a labour activist who fought for the rights of people in her beloved south Wales mining community, and there is a round-up of details relating to unclaimed estates

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: The documentary following British expats as they set up new businesses in Spain and France continues as novice B&B owners receive crucial feedback from their final guests of the season. Elsewhere, it remains to be seen whether weddings will make or break a Spanish B&B, and the Costa's newest fisherman sets out to make his dream a reality

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Charing, Kent, where landlady Natalie Palmer hopes to show that her B&B, The Oak, is better than any of her guests' establishments. Everything starts off well when a couple are delighted to have enough room for their yoga mats, but the rest of the party are unimpressed with the extractor fans

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants visit the White Lady in Worstead, Norfolk, on their second night, where proprietor Dennis Gilligan tells his guests about a spooky presence that resides there. The guests get competitive in a round of clay shooting, and debate whether they believe in the existence of ghosts

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Cranleigh House B&B in Combe Martin, Devon, where former couple Katherine Armitage and Stewart Johnston offer alternative accommodation for those who enjoy yoga and are seeking spiritual enlightenment. They hope the group will send out energy for world peace and healing, but Mike and Denise question whether the whole thing is a joke

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike and Denise Mitchell welcome the contestants to Beaches B&B in Paignton, Devon, which only opened for business a few weeks ago. Fellow competitor Katherine puts the owners through their paces at breakfast when she asks for something off the menu, and the hosts are pleasantly surprised at feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together for one last time to discover what they have been paid, and the identity of the winner. The Oak owner Natalie wants to know why some of her guests were not enthused by her extractor fans, but she herself receives aggression from Dennis over her criticism of the White Lady. Katherine and Stuart defend their vegetarian breakfast, and Mike and Denise deal with negative comments about their cereal selection

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The race is on to find heirs to the £100,000 estate of a woman from Southsea, and the search uncovers the romantic story of a husband and wife who met while serving in the Navy. Plus, an heir seeks to learn more about her aunts and uncles and makes a poignant discovery about her mother's twin brother. The programme also provides details of other unclaimed estates

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: It's mating season for Chester Zoo's onagers and after two years living alone, male Holmes is released into the female paddock, where he chases down every female in sight. Galapagos tortoise Charlie is losing weight and keepers believe that it's down to bullying from her older sister, so they decide to step in with an innovative system to try and alleviate the problem

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Krys have their first gastronomic weekend, and the pressure is on to make sure it runs smoothly. Belinda and Lee are hosting their biggest event so far with music, dancing and a slap-up meal for paying guests from the surrounding towns and villages. However, it seems they might have underestimated the wine requirements of the British contingent. Dick Strawbridge visits Angela and Steve to lend a hand with a precariously dislodged beam holding up the roof of one of their chateau turrets

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Harry and Briony Anscombe, who have decided to move from London to Cornwall with their three young children and begin building work on an ambitious house, inspired by American modernist properties. With a budget of just £400,000, Harry opts to project manage himself, paying local builders by the hour, but his lack of experience in construction proves to be problematic

programme.title: Ice Cold Murders: Rocco Schiavone
programme.desc: After getting into trouble in Rome when he took on a well-connected crook, cranky detective Rocco Schiavone is posted to the small ski town of Aosta. Rocco's detecting ability is allied with a delight in bending rules and a desire to chase women, but when operators turn up a disfigured body on the ski slopes, he discovers that his usual methods may not help him in this unknown locale. Rocco's troubles mount when he is stuck with a sidekick, Italo Pierron, who cannot bear the sight of dead bodies. Crime thriller, starring Marco Giallini and Ernesto D'Argenio. In Italian

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients include 84-year-old Neil who has fallen down the stairs at home, and his wife Jenny reflects on the shock of realising that their life may never be the same again. Orthopaedic registrar Sadia is concerned about a serious leg fracture to 25-year-old Danny, who was knocked off his motorbike, and may have other hidden internal injuries

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Niki, 34, is airlifted to St George's after crashing into another rider during a charity bike ride between London and Brighton. While staff try to contact Niki's wife Phill, who is almost 300 miles away at their home in the north east of England, 90-year-old Peter is rushed to A&E with severe blood poisoning

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

Orthopaedic registrar Sadia is concerned about a serious leg fracture to 25-year-old Danny, who was knocked off his motorbike, and may have other hidden internal injuries

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

Matt Tebbutt visits the UK's largest leek producer, where he's shocked by how much of the vegetable goes to waste, but amazed by a mobile factory on wheels that can process 60,000 leeks a day

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Last Stop Garage
programme.desc: The boys build an all-season fan boat for a local hunter so that he can travel over thin ice and shallow water

programme.title: Last Stop Garage
programme.desc: The CRB crew builds a barge to help the flooded village of Mud Lake recover from disaster

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike Wolfe sets his sights on a 1930s Cord convertible, and meets a retired auctioneer passing on the tricks of the trade to his daughter

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: US host Sean Kelly oversees Hampshire bidders battling to win potential treasures found in storage containers, lock-ups and garages

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: US host Sean Kelly oversees bidders in Preston, where Heavy D's gamble might just leave him with the hump, and viewers see if Jesse has what it takes to win big in the UK

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Frank finds a huge stash of beer-related memorabilia from an Oktoberfest fan, while Mike makes an offer on a ultra-rare Edison invention

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys brave a collection that sends chills up the spine at a former petrol station transformed into an eccentric home

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Hugh Dennis undertake a perilous journey through the Peruvian Andes. Their route takes in 2,000ft drops, one-lane dirt roads, remote mountain trails and muddy, humid jungle terrain, as they search for the lost city of Constitucion, once destined to be the capital of Peru

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond try to solve the problems that increasingly complex cars pose to an ageing population. They design a vehicle for elderly people and put it to the test in Dorset with the help of three pensioners. Meanwhile, James May gets acquainted with a revamped Range Rover in London before pitting the luxurious off-roader against an autonomous military machine in Nevada. Actor James McAvoy tackles the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters embark on a road trip across Australia's Northern Territory in fast, luxurious GT cars, with Jeremy Clarkson choosing a BMW M6 Gran Coupe, Richard Hammond picking a Bentley Continental GT V8 and James May opting for a Nissan GT-R. The trio set out on an adventure that takes in crocodile-infested rivers, a race around a vast opencast mine and an encounter with one of the world's longest lorries, concluding with an unusual challenge involving a farm, their cars and 4,000 cows. Plus, 24 star Kiefer Sutherland takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Mediterranean Escapes
programme.desc: The chef's final trip takes him to Morocco, where he samples the delights of couscous, tagines and mint tea in the country's bazaars, before travelling along the old silk route in south-eastern Turkey to taste kebabs, another of the Mediterranean's favourite foods

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to improve the ambulance, with the presenters all going their own separate ways to enhance one area they think could do with an overhaul. James focuses on passenger comfort, Richard tries to shorten response times by getting other drivers out of the way more effectively, an issue Jeremy tackles with the invention of what he calls the 'sports ambulance'. Their three very different creations are then put to the test with a series of severe and often messy challenges. Plus, Formula One driver Daniel Ricciardo is the Star in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson tests the new BMW M4 at the track, before switching to the German manufacturer's hybrid-powered i8 for a trip up the A1 to Whitby, North Yorkshire, where he decides which of the two he would rather take for the drive home. With Land Rover production ceasing in the UK later this year, Richard Hammond pays tribute to the 4x4 by attempting to drive one straight up the side of a dam, while James May puts the Mercedes AMG GT through its paces. Taking to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car are Will Smith and Margot Robbie

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The Taskmaster Greg Davies sends fear coursing through the veins of James Acaster, Jessica Knappett, Kerry Godliman, Phil Wang and Rhod Gilbert. In this edition, the challenges include an attempt to cheer up a grumpy traffic warden

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Olympic long jump champion Greg Rutherford, broadcaster Kirsty Young, actress-writer Joanna Scanlan and comedian Henning Wehn join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the panel show, trying to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon hosts

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites singer Geri Horner and comedians Katherine Ryan and Adam Buxton to express their anger over individuals, events and objects that upset them, hoping to curry the host's favour and see them consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include phone calls, pens that fail to work properly and marathons

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites comedian and writer Roisin Conaty, footballer-turned-pundit Chris Kamara and comedian and actor Nish Kumar to express their anger over individuals, events and objects that upset them, hoping to curry the host's favour and see them consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include bread at restaurants, downloading music and ex-partners

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Joe Lycett, Phil Wang and Ellie Taylor join regular panellist Alan Davies, as questionmaster Sandi Toksvig looks at the pastimes of past times. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the panellists attempt to answer the fiendishly difficult questions, with points invariably awarded for the answers the questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian is in the Canadian Arctic for this festive special of the travelogue in which he explores countries rarely visited by tourists. He wants to know whether there is more to this area than the prospect of freezing to death or being eaten by a polar bear. Travelling across frozen wastelands with host Johnny Issaluk, he spends a week in the largest territory, getting to grips with the Inuit hunter's way of life - not an easy task for vegan Romesh. He also witnesses the erosion and resurgence of Inuit traditions and discover why alcohol is often restricted in the communities

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: On Christmas Eve, Lee and Lucy - who is three days overdue with their first baby - find themselves mistakenly caught up in a robbery at a local department store. Alongside them are Lee's dad, Frank, dressed as Santa's elf, Daisy and neighbours Toby and Anna. Locked in and faced with a dangerous armed robber wearing a Father Christmas mask, they have to come up with an escape plan. But will Lee's poorly conceived ideas get them into even more trouble? One-off Christmas special of the comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: History Hunters
programme.desc: Documentary following father-and-son duo Dave and Steve Nuwar, who run a military antiques business. In the first edition, Dave takes a gamble when he makes one of his most expensive purchases ever. And with finances tight, the team head to Stratford fair to try and sell some treasures

programme.title: History Hunters
programme.desc: Dave and Steve spot something special at auction, but they're not the only ones on the trail of this First World War pilot's logbook

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary profile of the Queen's sister, whose life and loves reflected the social and sexual revolution that transformed Britain during the 20th century. With archive footage and interviews, the series follows Margaret's life as she redefined the image of the modern princess, and begins with her birth in 1930. The abdication of her uncle propelled Margaret to second in line to the throne, but her life would be shaped by dramatic social change as Britain entered a more egalitarian era after the Second World War

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: The discovery of a wreck off the coast of Florida sends a team of underwater explorers on a mission to discover the identity of a ship that simply shouldn't exist

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: In 1925 the SS Cotopaxi carrying coal and 32 men vanished. It has since become synonymous with one of the ocean's greatest mysteries - the Bermuda Triangle

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures once more on to the European rail network to retrace journeys featured in George Bradshaw's 1913 publication Continental Guide, beginning by travelling through Russia. He starts in the industrial city of Tula, before visiting Leo Tolstoy's former home of Yasnaya Polyana and learning how the author's life and works were intertwined with the railways. Michael then boards the train that runs from the Caspian Sea to Moscow, where he performs an important role in a dramatic opera at the Bolshoi Theatre, before exploring the beauty and history of St Petersburg and riding on the first railway ever built in Russia

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Pembroke Castle in South Wales, where objects brought in to be valued include a collection of intimate photographs charting the last days of the imprisoned Russian royal family in 1917, and a stick pin presented to one of George V's servants. A dairy maid demonstrates how to use a butter maker with a connection to Queen Victoria

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris travels 1,500 miles in a journey from Cape Town in South Africa to the Victoria Falls in Zimbabwe, following a hugely ambitious railway pioneered by 19th-century British colonial politician Cecil Rhodes. He starts his trip in luxury on the Blue Train, a five-star hotel on rails that takes him to the mining town of Kimberley, where he explores the diamond mine where Rhodes made his fortune

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: In North Wales a secret facility which once manufactured mustard gas and enriched uranium lies in ruins. In Malta is a luxurious quarantine facility, that hides a dark secret

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Ruins of a vast industrial ghost town in the United States, a windswept Scottish island and the remains of a resort in Japan that watched a city burn

programme.title: Hitler's Most Wanted
programme.desc: As Hitler's personal lawyer, Hans Frank twisted the law to help the Führer's agenda, and became known as the Butcher of Poland. James finds out how he became a power-hungry monster

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: This edition examines the efforts of the British and Canadians who fought elite German divisions to capture the city of Caen, the key to breaking out of Normandy

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Pembroke Castle in South Wales, where objects brought in to be valued include a collection of intimate photographs charting the last days of the imprisoned Russian royal family in 1917, and a stick pin presented to one of George V's servants. A dairy maid demonstrates how to use a butter maker with a connection to Queen Victoria

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Steered by his 1913 Bradshaw railway guide, Michael Portillo takes the train down the spine of Italy as he travels from Rome to Sicily. He begins by weaving among the capital city's landmarks on the back of a 1950s Vespa, before boarding the train south to Naples, where he finds out about the first railway to be built in the country, and ventures into the crater of Mount Vesuvius. He takes a detour to the island of Capri, before finishing his journey in the ancient hilltop town of Taormina

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: The Wieliczka Salt mine in southern Poland, some 327 metres deep, where the voids left behind from excavating salt have been transformed into extraordinary spaces, carved by hand

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Philippa has a bright idea for raising the spirits of the kitchen staff. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood and Thelma Barlow

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: The girls celebrate Japanese-style at their work party, and Bren enters right into the spirit of things - but not as much as her mother, who turns up unexpectedly. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood and Julie Walters

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Morale plummets to its lowest point in the canteen with Tony in hospital and Dolly and Jean staging a walk-out, leaving Bren to run the graveyard shift single-handedly on the most important night of the year. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood, Andrew Dunn, Shobna Gulati and Celia Imrie

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: René hides his gold in the cuckoo clock, then at the Colonel's order disguises himself and his Resistance friends as a string quartet at the chateau

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Edith accepts Monsieur Alfonse's proposal of marriage, much to the dismay of Rene, who realises he stands to lose the cafe if she ties the knot

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Yvette and Edith both question René about the whereabouts of the gold, while the Colonel and Gruber search the café for the missing Enigma machine

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The incredible efforts to ensure that the hastily constructed temporary fix around the damaged reactor at Chernobyl becomes a permanent solution, known as the New Safe Confinement

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: How a team of intrepid engineers keep the London Array, one of the largest offshore wind farms on the planet, maintained and producing electricity whatever the weather

programme.title: History Hunters
programme.desc: A former dealer is selling his entire medal collection - will Dave and Steve find anything worth bidding for? And Steve digs deep into a WW1 army medic's family history

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part two of two. Experts analyse the Princess's marriage to Lord Snowdon at the start of the 1960s. At home with celebrities and artists, they're riding the wave of a cultural and sexual revolution that's transforming Britain. The mystique of monarchy is being increasingly undermined by the emergence of a more open and egalitarian society

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: Divers search a newly discovered wreck as they investigate the story of the SS Justicia, sunk in the First World War by U-Boats off the coast of Ireland

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: A marine biologist and a deep-sea diver explore Chuuk Lagoon, one of the world's greatest underwater graveyards, for a wreck discovered by sonar nearly 20 years ago

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Steered by his 1913 Bradshaw railway guide, Michael Portillo takes the train down the spine of Italy as he travels from Rome to Sicily. He begins by weaving among the capital city's landmarks on the back of a 1950s Vespa, before boarding the train south to Naples, where he finds out about the first railway to be built in the country, and ventures into the crater of Mount Vesuvius. He takes a detour to the island of Capri, before finishing his journey in the ancient hilltop town of Taormina

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce returns for a second episode from Pembroke Castle in West Wales. Items brought in to be valued include a sapphire ring that changes colour in different light, a papal hat that once belonged to Pope Pius XII, and a collection of Anglo-Indian paintings from 1780 that were once used to decorate a village's scout hut

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris travels along the route of the Soviet-era Trans-Caucasus Railway, beginning in the Azerbaijani capital of Baku. He then heads into Georgia, exploring Tbilisi's vintage metro system and funicular railway, before travelling to the holiday resort of Borjomi, where world-class engineering is on display in a mountain rail bridge designed by Gustave Eiffel

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An engineering masterpiece carved into the cliff-face, and a long-range beacon that transmitted one of the most infamous messages in American history

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An American desert canyon where fortunes were made and lives were lost, and an island fortress in Scotland with an Achilles heel

programme.title: Hitler's Most Wanted

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide to explore Poland. He begins in the capital Warsaw, where he takes to the floor to dance to one of Chopin's polonaises with high-school students rehearsing for their leavers' ball, before discovering how the former industrial city of Lodz supplied the vast Russian empire of the early 20th century. He then heads to Poznan and rides one of the few remaining steam-powered commuter trains, visits a factory in Wroclaw that manufactures car bodies for locomotives, and ends his journey in Krakow, where he takes a tour in an iconic vehicle of the communist era

programme.title: War for the Planet of the Apes (2017)
programme.desc: A devastating conflict erupts between humans and intelligent primates in a post-apocalyptic future. The leader of the apes is determined to make peace and prove his people are not savages - but a tragic course of events leaves him wrestling with a desire for revenge. Sci-fi adventure sequel, the conclusion of the trilogy of prequels to the 1968 movie. Starring Andy Serkis, Woody Harrelson and Steve Zahn

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson meets 21-year-old YouTuber Jay from London, who has no problem messaging women online but cannot make connections in real life, and 28-year-old Victoria from Liverpool, who is looking for her very own Xena, Warrior Princess. Will the back-to-basics approach of Naked Attraction help either of them find what they're looking for?

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson presents the dating show with a difference, in which another two singletons take their pick of potential partners based purely on their naked bodies. First up is 27-year-old Kelly, a carer from Brighton. She's unsure whether she's looking for a man or a woman but hopes to find her the perfect partner to give her a little TLC. She is followed by 29-year-old steeplejack Carlo from Somerset, who is looking for woman that he can sweep off her feet

programme.title: Guy Martin's Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Part two of two. Guy's training ramps up at the UK's only aerial combat school, where he must master dogfighting at 200mph, handling extreme g-force, and flying upside down. He enlists the help of the Parachute Regiment for a unique experiment demonstrating the firepower of Second World War aircraft machine guns, before being allowed behind the joystick of the world's only two-seater Hawker Hurricane to face a Messerschmitt 109 in the skies over Kent

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Self-made millionaire Delia is looking for a man to build a future with - could City trader Mark be the one? Bengy has a trauma from his past he is reluctant to share - luckily Lara is a caring soul with a listening ear. butcher Nick is excited at the thought of meeting someone, even though he admits he doesn't know how to speak to women

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Architecture student Priti has trouble finding a man in the academic world and wants someone older, rugged and tattooed. No stranger to the tattooist's chair is Australian entrepreneur Nick - but will the age gap end up being a problem for these two singletons? Firefighter Katie and her date - also called Katie - open up about significant turning points in their lives, while superhero fanatic Richard thinks he has hit the jackpot when his date turns out to be a self-confessed geek and Comic Con fan

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth and final visit is to the Ladbrooke Hotel in the heart of Birmingham, where manager Snehal Lakhani claims he accepts nothing less than perfection. A room with a broken window proves far from flawless for Natasha and Laura, while Danni and Graham are concerned about the proximity of a train line to their bedroom

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Road Trip
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix heads to the Midi-Pyrénées region of south-west France. He meets two young families from the Midlands setting up a cycling business, a London couple who are running a glamping ground and an ex-army major and his wife who are dealing with a big renovation project

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Debbie and Dave from West Sussex fancy owning a holiday home in the Dordogne region of France, so Jonnie Irwin lines up five properties within their £200,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps Glamorgan-based couple Richard and Felicity Arcelli search for a second home in the Coimbra region of Portugal, with the pair hoping to find a renovation project within their £40,000 budget that can be transformed into a permanent residence

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Surrey couple Brenda and Nick Kennett are looking for a family holiday home in the Correze region of France, and Sara Damergi takes them to view potential properties within their budget of £60,000

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Banker Mandeep Rajput is keen to impress as he hosts the first dinner party in Dudley, West Midlands, joined by musical pensioner Di Dawson, quiz master Adam Reece, dog groomer Vicky Pell and businesswoman Sarah Freeman

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pub quiz host Adam Reece takes charge of the second dinner party in Dudley, West Midlands, hoping his 'mixed up' theme, Italian menu and game show-style entertainment will be enough to win him the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pensioner Di Dawson hosts the third dinner party from Dudley, West Midlands, and hopes some good honest home cooking will impress the guests. However, the evening is overshadowed by Adam Reece's offensive humour and his flirtatious behaviour with dog groomer Vicky Pell

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog groomer Vicky Pell hosts a cruise ship-themed party for her guests in Dudley in the West Midlands, but Adam, whose smutty sense of humour has caused outrage all week, continues to behave badly. His actions eventually create a rift with Di, threatening to turn the evening sour

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businesswoman Sarah Freeman hosts the final dinner party from Dudley, West Midlands, and hopes to impress her guests with a Moroccan menu, but the simmering tension between rivals Adam Reece and Di Dawson threatens to jeopardise her chances of winning the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Beaver, Pennsylvania, where he meets a brother-and-sister team who find it almost impossible to work together. Their relationship is so fractured that they're failing to save their father's Italian restaurant Levanti's from being overwhelmed by local competition, and the situation has become so desperate that their dad is in danger of losing his life savings

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Carol longs to buy a holiday home for her and her husband on the island of Lanzarote that will work for all four generations of their family. She particularly loves the east coast, with its buzzing resorts of Costa Teguise and Puerto del Carmen, and is looking for a town house or apartment with a pool and a view from the terrace. With a budget of £150,000 - and Carol's daughter-in-law-Donna coming along to help - Jasmine Harman sets out to find a property to please everyone

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: James first visited Crete over 40 years ago and has always longed to buy a holiday home there. Now that his partner Alison has also fallen in love with the Greek island too, they've both decided to turn their dream into a reality. Ben Hillman helps the couple find a suitable holiday home - ideally a traditional village house - on their budget of £80,000

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill, actor-presenter-musician Martin Kemp, comedian and actress Roisin Conaty and broadcaster Bill Turnbull take part in the charity contest. Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding guide them through the assorted challenges, and Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith pass judgment on their baking skills

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two couples searching in Sheffield. Scientists Lois and Ahmed's strategic approach has so far seen them lose out on eight houses, being outbid by just a few thousand pounds each time. With a toddler in tow, and living with Lois's parents, the heat is on for Kirstie to find their own family home. Phil is with Liz and Luke, who are set on the rural areas around the city, close to the Peak District

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on the rising incidence of theft in Northamptonshire, where in 2018 burglaries hit a five-year high. PC Imogen Robinson chases a prolific shoplifter who has stolen a box of chocolate bars from a shop. PC Ben Norris is dispatched to apprehend a suspected burglar who then carries out a rooftop protest

programme.title: Baby Surgeons: Delivering Miracles
programme.desc: Documentary following patients and staff at one of the UK's leading fetal medicine units. This episode follows three expectant mothers navigating complex pregnancies. Becky's unborn baby has a rare lung tumour that is growing rapidly, Ann-Marie is carrying identical twins, one of which has selective growth restriction jeopardising both baby's chances of surviving, and Randika's dwarfism makes giving birth a difficult proposition

programme.title: Paramedics: Britain's Lifesavers
programme.desc: Documentary following West Midlands Ambulance Service at the peak of the Covid-19 pandemic in April 2020, from the perspective of the crews, control room staff and managers as they deal with the human cost of the biggest ever challenge to the service. To get more ambulances onto the frontline, managers deploy student paramedics, with 21-year-old Sam and 20-year-old Caitlyn encountering patients with breathing difficulties, in care homes, and with mental health issues that appear isolation-related

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack, Mike Wozniak and Sarah Kendall continue their quest to curry favour with omnipotent Taskmaster Greg Davies. Lee attacks a toy vole and Sarah invents a sweet dressing for an unusual salad. Meanwhile, Charlotte provides surprising sound effects for a set of Russian dolls. Greg's assistant Alex Horne takes notes, records times and offers the contestants some much-needed moral support

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Paul Foot take on Jon Richardson and Angela Barnes in the words-and-numbers game, while poet John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: The Secret Lives of Slim People
programme.desc: Sabrina Grant examines the lives of people who do not stick to a fitness regimen or seem to worry about what they eat, but do not have a problem with their weight and stay trim without trying. With the help of private detectives and secret cameras the programme examines their every move and meal. The first edition features teaching assistant and mum-of-three Hayley from Eastbourne, an unashamed chocoholic with a busy lifestyle, and Rinia from Surrey, who wonders why she manages to stay in shape in spite of her diet of takeaways

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits India on the trail of turmeric and finds himself at a wedding ceremony where guests are showered with the exotic spice. Kate Quilton finds out how much milk goes into a bar of chocolate, while Andi Oliver uncovers the reason why asparagus delivers some of its eaters with very pungent urine

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the final day of the competition, Churchill's Hotel's Jeff Brown and Vicky O'Loughlin are first to face the feedback and Jeff's keen to turn down the heating woes. Plus, Snehal from the Ladbrooke Hotel has a bone to pick with all of the B&Bers. After some frank discussions, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Road Trip
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix is in the Costa del Sol, meeting a chef from Scotland with a keen eye for fresh Mediterranean seafood, two sea lovers running a boat charter business and a former journalist from London on the hunt for local underwater treasures

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Lancashire couple Derek and Jane find a dream home in Chiclana de la Frontera, Spain, showing them five properties to accommodate all their grown-up children

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi offers more advice to people buying a second property abroad, this time helping to find the perfect holiday home to be shared by three sisters and their brother in the Poitou-Charentes region of south-western France

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sama Damergi helps Paula Brown to pursue her dream of retiring to the Vendee region of western France. With a budget of £80,000, Paula hopes she will be able to buy a property in which she can keep chickens and grow vegetables

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Health-conscious grandmother Tina Ainsworth hosts the first dinner party in Mansfield, Nottinghamshire, putting on a Chinese-themed evening for her guests - croupier Tomasz Wisniewski, PR consultant Julia Smith, copy writer Rachel Imms and DJ Gareth Harding

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Croupier Tomasz Wisniewski hosts the second dinner party in Mansfield, Nottinghamshire. His 'outrageous'-themed evening leaves plenty of scope for his guests' imaginations, but his balloon-pumping competition brings out the competitive streak in one contestant

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Gareth Harding hosts the third dinner party from Mansfield, Nottinghamshire, where he provides an Ibiza-themed night after he transforms his garden into a Balearic cocktail bar and club complete with lasers and disco lights. The guests seem impressed with the host's sophisticated menu, but he may have bitten off more than he can chew as he attempts to cook a tricky beef Wellington

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Animal lover Rachel Imms hosts the fourth party of the week from Mansfield. She opts for a French-themed evening, but her complicated main course proves to be a challenge, and her accordionist entertainer does not appeal to everyone in the way she had hoped

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dance fanatic Julia Smith hosts the final party in Mansfield, Nottinghamshire, and is confident she will wow fellow competitors with her fancy house and hospitality, having roped in two waiters to give the evening a touch of class. A spot of salsa dancing proves entertaining for all the wrong reasons, and as the event draws to a close, the contestants gather to discover which dinner was rated the best

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sam's Mediterranean Kabob Room in Monrovia, California, and within minutes of his arrival, is caught in the crossfire of an argument between the owner, his wife and their children, all of whom are feeling the pressure of working seven days a week. Can the chef save the business and mend the warring family's frayed relationships?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Brothers Peter and David share a home in Wolverhampton and have decided to jointly buy a holiday home in the Spanish town of Torrevieja. With a budget of £100,000, they want a home that not only ticks all their boxes but also suits their large extended family too, so turn to Jean Johansson for advice

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas helps a couple from Kent find a home on Spain's Orihuela Costa on a budget of £100,000, with a golf course within easy reach

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Will Hardie and George struggle to bring ancient Roman technology back to life and the build on their log cabin in the wilderness begins in earnest, while the road trip around Italy takes in an impressive fire station. The architect also meets David Moreton, a former Paralympic swimmer hoping to turn an old air ambulance into an amphibious crash pad for his charity fundraising tours, and DIY enthusiasts Ben and Michelle, who want to build a summerhouse for free, using hundreds of disused shipping pallets

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets newlyweds who are investing their life savings in a dilapidated 1950s yacht, and in rural Aberdeenshire, a barley farmer who wants to build a traditional Highland bothy for just £8,000. The Italian road trip visits a stunning treehouse in an olive grove, while George and Will's home-made wilderness lodge starts to take shape, with the roof ready to be installed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two couples searching in Sheffield. programme.title: Trip Hazard: My Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Rosie Jones is joined by fellow comedian Jenny Eclair for an 'outdoorsy' adventure on the island of Anglesey in north Wales. The pair discover they'd make terrible farmers when they try their hand at sheep herding and visit the island's many Neolithic stone sites. Rosie takes Jenny on a horse ride along the waterfront, and they take to the skies on the world's fastest zip wire. As the sun sets, they meet a coast craft expert, who teaches them essential survival skills on one of the island's many picturesque beaches - and it's quickly evident they'd not last more than few hours if left to fend for themselves. Narrated by Olivia Colman

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: Alex, who drives a £40,000 Mercedes-Benz car, goes to stay with Jen, Antony and baby Jaxon, who survive on £1,000 a month in Bolton

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of medical emergency teams in the A&E department of Barnsley Hospital, beginning as severe weather conditions place it under even more stress than usual. Dr Julian tends to pensioner Brian who was brought in after breaking his ankle walking his dog in the snow, and the medic also sees former miner Andrew, who has a chronic lung condition. Paediatric consultant Dr Jane is called into action when four-year-old Teddy is rushed in after gashing his head in a sledging accident

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: Sunday's services grind to a halt when the 11.57 service to Penzance derails as it is departing, while a knife-carrying passenger causes concern in Bristol. Elsewhere, a team of specialists clean the mats laid down to protect the tracks from the actions of passengers ignoring the signs advising them not to flush onboard toilets while the trains are in the station

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Following the hidden army of enforcement teams and inspectors out to nail the litter louts and the fearless sewage warriors clearing the mess

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Ben face a challenging eviction in London, where a vulnerable tenant in rent arrears has barricaded her front door, and the agents receive a tirade of abuse. Meanwhile, Brian and Del chase a debt of nearly £2,000 in Surrey, before trying to recover almost £3,000 in Northamptonshire. Finally, Steve and Ru head to Portsmouth to evict a young family for a landlord who wants her property back

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Two known offenders end up in trouble again after a high-speed chase. An encounter with a gang of hoodies leads to a close shave for one officer, another goes in pursuit of a four-legged fugitive, and a Lamborghini turns out to be not all it seems

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: An officer is nearly run over by a desperate driver, and another's patience is tested by a drunken motorist. Plus, a law enforcer goes in search of a suspicious character, and a group of youths tries to deceive the interception unit

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A helicopter is used in the pursuit of an armed robber, ending in a serious accident. A sniffer dog makes a discovery, a fraudster is taken into custody and a gang of lorry thieves is pursued in a major manhunt. Plus, new recruits at South Yorkshire's Road Crime Unit are put to the test by a pair of suspected burglars

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Steve have an emotional confrontation in Berkshire when a tearful woman accuses her landlord of fleecing her for extra rent, while Brian and Del travel to Luton to recover £4,500 owed by an elusive debtor who has been giving them the runaround

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: One-off edition in which the High Court enforcement agents are caught in the crossfire of three bitter family disputes, including a debt-dodging man who pushes his panic-stricken mother over the edge. Plus, a family booted onto the streets at night by a ruthless aunt, and a man who won't engage with the agents and loses his car

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Bea threatens to go straight to the publisher, bypassing Olivia in an attempt to force her to change her statement. Yashvi considers moving to Sydney, leaving Ned crushed

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Justin and Leah's relationship strains with the loss of their house. Chloe and Ryder clash over whether to go public about their relationship, and Christian is back at work

programme.title: Inside Iceland: Britain's Budget Supermarket
programme.desc: A current area for growth is vegan food, so Iceland's vegan buyer visits a factory to watch meatless sausage rolls being produced, as well as scoping out vegan products for the future. The Iceland team also tucks into its rivals' choc ices, while out in Italy the team develops a new ice-cream cake. Elsewhere, some environmentally friendly packaging for fruit and vegetables is put to the test with a view to making the company plastic free by 2023

programme.title: Bargain Brits on Benefits
programme.desc: Cameras follow folks whose clever ideas and savvy schemes save them a small fortune

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Richard and Brennan come to the aid of a compulsive buyer in Morecambe whose semi-detached bungalow has become overrun with junk, dust and grime

programme.title: The Woman with 40 Cats
programme.desc: Documentary giving an insight into the lives of people whose love of animals has become an obsession. Retired secretary Marlene has 40 cats, which are groomed and bathed every day, while Deb and her family share their home with a brood of chickens. Matt keeps a huge collection of snakes, lizards and other reptiles, and he must also breed thousands of insects and worms to keep the creatures fed

programme.title: Gypsy Kids: Our Secret World
programme.desc: Childhood in gypsy and traveller communities. Eight-year-old Ellie-May has been teased at school for being a gypsy. Now, armed with her fake tan and sequinned dresses, she is hoping to shine as she takes on her rivals in a beauty contest. Fifteen-year-old David works on his haggling skills, while young Nicole dreams of stepping away from her domestic duties to sing in a talent show, organised by her grandad Paddy Doherty, of Celebrity Big Brother fame

programme.title: Good Will Hunting (1997)
programme.desc: A troubled maths genius refuses to use his gift, preferring to work as a janitor and lead a normal life with his friends. Eventually, a university professor persuades him to see a psychologist, who befriends him and proves the only person able to help him come to terms with his talent and past traumas. Drama, written by and starring Matt Damon and Ben Affleck, who won Oscars for their screenplay. With Robin Williams, Minnie Driver and Stellan Skarsgard

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.desc: The stories of more budget lifestyles in the bright lights of Blackpool. Chambermaid-turned-B&B owner Claire faces a big challenge when a group of VIP business leaders book out her entire hotel. Glaswegian Sammy has lived in Blackpool for 33 years, and now he runs the only caravan site in the town centre, while Blondie tribute artist Fiona moved to the resort with dreams of making it big in the music industry, but has to travel miles out of town to get gigs
